methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
CarbonCapture Inc., a Series B–stage climate and energy technology company, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (Lanza Technologies, ticker LNZA) operating in the same sector. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $300M on $80M total raised.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The Stage-Progression method, using a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $570M from the $300M base. The Peer-Median method examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M)—yielding a median of $400M. This peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $120M–$5.0B), reflecting heterogeneity typical of frontier-energy investments.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $340M (low) to $656M (high), with a median of $485M. The current valuation of $300M sits below this range, suggesting potential upside if performance meets sector expectations. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peers, the outsized influence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at the upper bound, and limited public comparables (n=1) to anchor absolute valuation. The analysis supports a post-money valuation materially above current levels, contingent on execution risk and technology validation.
